headline.text: L3 ML4 cold stamp jam held the line down *2.5 hours* overnight — motors wouldn't sync, escalated to engineering.

per_plant_summary.L1: Sollich 3 lost 1.5 hours on a C1 belt roller bearing failure — greased and reinstalled without the correct part, new bearings arrive Monday. Sollich 2 planning a reheat around 09:00 for enrober buildup. Sollich 4 had a quick metal detector air connector fix.
per_plant_summary.L2: ML3 paused briefly for a roof water leak, resolved by 12:15. ML2 lost startup time to flow wrap power issues and a loose Sweco that spilled chocolate — both cleared by mid-morning.
per_plant_summary.L3: ML4 had a rough night: 2.5-hour cold stamp jam (motors wouldn't sync), depositor 4 sensor failures forcing manual mode, a scratched north filter replacement, and product demolding issues at the end of the window. ML1 battled recurring high temper all day — cold water setpoint was wrong for days, mixing water dropped out of range overnight.

notes.text: MDLZ touring the plant tomorrow morning (per Charity Young 11:08).
S3 C1 belt roller bearing is a temporary fix — correct bearings ordered for Monday delivery.

resolved.title: S3 C1 belt roller bearing failure
resolved.summary: Roller after metal detector making grinding noise, eventual bearing failure, line down 02:38.
resolved.resolution: Line running at 04:30 — bearing removed, greased, reinstalled as temporary fix. Correct bearings ordered for Monday (Jody Howse).

resolved.title: S4 metal detector air connector broke
resolved.summary: Air fitting failed on metal detector during 1st shift.
resolved.resolution: Maintenance replaced connector, line running again (Marcos Jimenez).

resolved.title: ML3 roof water leak over hopper
resolved.summary: Water dripping from RTU near Z-belt and supersack hopper, risk of contamination.
resolved.resolution: Line back up at 12:15 — unhooked vent sock to prevent dripping, William draining sump and working on RTU. Sock reconnected by maintenance at 05:04 Friday.

resolved.title: ML2 flow wrap power failure
resolved.summary: Flow wrap would not turn on at 3rd shift startup.
resolved.resolution: Power restored by 05:42, centerline and print verified good (Fernando Flores).

resolved.title: ML3 line down for maintenance
resolved.summary: Brief unplanned stop, cause not specified in chat.

resolved.title: ML2 Sweco moved and spilled chocolate
resolved.summary: Sweco not tightened down enough after changeover, shifted during startup and spilled chocolate.
resolved.resolution: Sweco adjusted by 06:13, waiting on temper to stabilize before restart (Gustavo Rodas).

resolved.title: TG3 film jam and printer clog
resolved.summary: Film caught by fin wheels (excess glue buildup), printer wand clogged — TG3 paused for one hour starting 23:39.
resolved.resolution: Cleared at 23:46 — operators cleaned fin wheels and printer wand, "TG3 back up and running" (Kavin Ayala).

resolved.title: ML1 metal detector alarm
resolved.summary: Metal detector faulted out mid-shift, QA and maintenance called.
resolved.resolution: Line running again at 15:52 (cleared in ~7 minutes).

resolved.title: C6 belt tracking to north rail
resolved.summary: Belt repeatedly climbing into the rail on ML1 flow wrap, causing line stops throughout 2nd and 3rd shift.
resolved.resolution: Cleared at 03:33 — "C6 Belt is running good now" after maintenance adjustments.

resolved.title: North side chocolate filter scratched
resolved.summary: Filter on north Sweco screen found scratched during routine check, line stopped pumping chocolate to replace.
resolved.resolution: Screen replaced by 21:17, no metal hits reported, team monitoring (Angel Cabrera).

resolved.title: ML1 camera sensor fault at shift end
resolved.summary: Metal detector faulted at 06:17, traced to dirty camera sensor.
resolved.resolution: Sensor cleaned, fault cleared (Imperial and Jose Vasquez).

needs_attention.title: S2 enrober buildup, reheat planned
needs_attention.summary: Heavy buildup on enrober, 2 hours of caramel left, planning reheat around 09:00 and raising sump temp from 28°C to 30°C.
needs_attention.status: Reheat scheduled for ~09:00 this morning (07:05 update). Proactive stop to prevent quality issues (Justin Garner, Curt Willis).

needs_attention.title: ML2 twin belts and chicane belt installed backwards
needs_attention.summary: Belts put on reversed during changeover — wide twin belt on wrong side, first chicane belt backwards.
needs_attention.status: Identified at 05:54 by Justin Halladay, no follow-up on correction in chat.

needs_attention.title: ML1 recurring high temper — settings out of range
needs_attention.summary: Multiple high temper stops throughout 24h window (0.8-0.9 slope), traced to cold water at wrong setpoint for days and mixing water dropped to 79.7 (min is 81.0).
needs_attention.status: Cold water corrected to 57°F, mixing water being brought back to 80.5+. Temper yo-yoing overnight, adjustments ongoing by 3rd shift. Robert Villarreal coaching on parameter limits.

needs_attention.title: ML4 cold stamp jam — 2.5 hour downtime
needs_attention.summary: Mold jammed in cold stamp section at 01:19, motors wouldn't sync via gameboy, had to clear via HMI, escalated to Preston.
needs_attention.status: Line restarted at 03:17 after 2.5 hours. Root cause: mold not centered correctly. Gameboy control failure required HMI workaround (Kavin, Alan, Preston).

needs_attention.title: ML4 product demolding failure at window close
needs_attention.summary: Product coming out of molds but not demolding properly starting 07:03, worsening through end of window.
needs_attention.status: At 07:15 (window close): product demolding partially but not fully. Active issue, no resolution posted (Itzel Chavez).

needs_attention.title: ML4 depositor 4 hopper sensor failure
needs_attention.summary: Level sensors reading empty when full, causing auto-drain, multiple stoppages from 00:28 through 05:14.
needs_attention.status: Running on manual mode as of 05:14. Maintenance worked on sensors, issue persists. Line operating but requires manual hopper control (Kimberly Morales).
